perturbation.original_text: 1.21 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means, with respect to the efforts to be expended by a Party with respect to any objective (e.g., Development Activities and Commercialization hereunder), the level of efforts consistent with the efforts and resources [***] of similar market potential, at a similar stage in development or product lifecycle, taking into account the stage of development or product lifecycle of other of [***] product candidates, safety and efficacy, product profile, cost of goods, the competitiveness of the marketplace, such company's patent position with respect to such product (including such company's ability to obtain or enforce, or have obtained or enforced, such patent rights), the Third Party patent landscape relevant to the product, the regulatory structure involved, the likelihood of regulatory approval, the likelihood and extent of anticipated or actual profitability of the applicable product, and other technical, legal, scientific and medical considerations. Without limiting the foregoing, Commercially Reasonable Efforts requires, with respect to such obligations, that a Party: (i) promptly assign responsibility for such obligation to specific employee(s) who are held accountable for progress and monitor such progress on an on-going basis, (ii) set objectives for carrying out such obligations, and (iii) allocate resources designed to advance progress with respect to such objectives.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.21 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means, with respect to the efforts to be expended by a Party with respect to any objective (e.g., Development Activities and Commercialization hereunder), the level of efforts consistent with standard industry practices. A Party should consider the safety and efficacy and product profile. Without limiting the foregoing, Commercially Reasonable Efforts requires, with respect to such obligations, that a Party: (i) assign responsibility for such obligation to specific employee(s), (ii) set objectives for carrying out such obligations, and (iii) allocate resources to advance progress with respect to such objectives.
perturbation.explanation: By removing key elements of the definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts', such as considering market potential, stage of development, patent position, regulatory structure, and profitability, the modified definition becomes vague and subjective. This omission contradicts the detailed provisions in Section 3.1 which states that 'Aimmune, itself or with or through its Affiliates and Sublicensees, shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to perform the Development Activities for the Product to (i) achieve the development milestones set forth in Section 7.2, and (ii) obtain Regulatory Approval for the Product'. Without a clear definition, it is unclear how Aimmune will actually use Commercially Reasonable Efforts
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Overview of Development. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Aimmune shall be responsible for the Development of the Product as set forth herein. Aimmune, itself or with or through its Affiliates and Sublicensees, shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to perform the Development Activities for the Product to (i) achieve the development milestones set forth in Section 7.2, and (ii) obtain Regulatory Approval for the Product.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' with specific considerations such as market potential, stage of development, patent position, and profitability. The changed text significantly narrows this definition to simply 'standard industry practices' and consideration of 'safety and efficacy and product profile', removing the other key elements. This creates a contradiction with Section 3.1, which mandates Aimmune to use 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to achieve development milestones and obtain regulatory approval. The reduced definition makes it unclear how Aimmune's efforts will be evaluated against the more comprehensive criteria originally intended, thus hindering the enforceability of Section 3.1 and creating a direct conflict regarding the standard of effort required.

perturbation.original_text: 1.60 "Product" means any biopharmaceutical product containing or comprising (i) the Antibody; and (ii) any Variant of the Antibody that: (a) [***] and (b) [***]; provided, that a Product does not include any Active Ingredient that is [***], other than the Antibody as described in the foregoing subsections (i) and (ii). For clarity, Product excludes: (1) [***]; (2) [***]; (3) [***]; (4) [***]; (5) [***]; or (6) [***].
perturbation.changed_text: 1.60 "Product" means any substance containing the Antibody; it must be sold under Aimmune's trademark.
perturbation.explanation: By simplifying the definition of 'Product' and removing the detailed conditions and exclusions, the modified definition now only focuses on 'any substance containing the Antibody' and that it must be sold under Aimmune's trademark. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 5.4.1 which states that 'Aimmune shall Commercialize the Product under the trademark and the trade dress selected by Aimmune (the "Product Trademark" and the "Product Trade Dress", respectively)'. The original definition included specific parameters related to the antibody, variants, and active ingredients, while the modified version reduces this to any substance containing the antibody, sold under a particular trademark. This creates ambiguity regarding what qualifies as a 'Product' under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4 Product Trademarks and Product Trade Dress. 5.4.1 Product Trademark. Aimmune shall Commercialize the Product under the trademark and the trade dress selected by Aimmune (the "Product Trademark" and the "Product Trade Dress", respectively).
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a detailed definition of 'Product', specifying components, variants, and exclusions. The changed text simplifies this definition to 'any substance containing the Antibody; it must be sold under Aimmune's trademark'. This creates a direct conflict with Section 5.4.1, which requires Aimmune to 'Commercialize the Product under the trademark and the trade dress selected by Aimmune'. The altered definition removes the specific criteria and exclusions that defined 'Product' originally, and now the term 'Product' is too broad, causing a contradiction with Section 5.4.1 because the definition of product is unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 9.3.2 Xencor Product Specific Patent, Aimmune Patents and Joint Collaboration Patents. (a) Aimmune shall have the first right to prepare, file, prosecute and maintain (i) Xencor Product Specific Patents, (ii) Aimmune Patents Covering an Antibody or Product, and (iii) Joint Collaboration Patents, [***], including by conducting reissues, reexaminations, interferences, and/or defending against post grant proceedings, such as inter partes reviews and oppositions and other challenges to the validity or enforceability of the relevant Patent; provided that Aimmune shall receive Xencor's prior written approval, not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed, before conducting reissues, reexaminations, interferences, and/or defending against post grant proceedings for the [***], such as inter partes reviews and oppositions and other challenges to the validity or enforceability of such relevant Patent. [***]. [***]. Aimmune shall keep Xencor informed of the status of Xencor Product Specific Patents, Aimmune Patents Covering an Antibody or Product, and Joint Collaboration Patents [***]. With respect to any material substantive submissions that Aimmune is required to or otherwise intends to submit to a patent office with respect to a [***], Aimmune shall provide a draft of such submission to Xencor at least [***] ([***]) days (or such time as is possible) prior to the deadline for, or the intended filing date of, such submission, whichever is earlier (or as soon as reasonably possible if Aimmune has less than [***] ([***]) days' notice of a deadline for submission). Xencor shall have the right to review and comment upon any such submission by Aimmune to a patent office, and will provide such comments within [***] ([***]) days after receiving such submission (provided, that if no comments are received within such [***] ([***]) day period, then Aimmune may proceed with such submission). Aimmune shall [***]any suggestions or recommendations of Xencor concerning the preparation, filing, prosecution and maintenance thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.3.2 Xencor Product Specific Patent, Aimmune Patents and Joint Collaboration Patents. (a) Aimmune shall have the right to file and maintain (i) Xencor Product Specific Patents, (ii) Aimmune Patents Covering an Antibody or Product, and (iii) Joint Collaboration Patents. Aimmune shall keep Xencor informed of the status of Xencor Product Specific Patents, Aimmune Patents Covering an Antibody or Product, and Joint Collaboration Patents. Aimmune shall provide a draft of such submission to Xencor prior to the deadline. Xencor shall have the right to review and comment upon any such submission by Aimmune to a patent office. Aimmune shall consider suggestions or recommendations of Xencor concerning the preparation, filing, prosecution and maintenance thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the conditions related to prior written approval for reissues, reexaminations, interferences, and defending against post-grant proceedings for the 'Xencor Product Specific Patents, Aimmune Patents and Joint Collaboration Patents'. This contradicts section 9.3.1 which gives the authority for Xencor General Patents to only Xencor. The change omits important details of the patent process and could create disputes over patent strategy. This creates ambiguity over the levels of involvement for each side of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.3.1 Xencor General Patents. Subject to, and without limiting Aimmune's rights under, Section 9.4 of this Agreement, Xencor shall have the sole right to prepare, file, prosecute and maintain all Xencor General Patents, [***], including by conducting reissues, reexaminations, interferences, and/or defending against post grant proceedings, such as inter partes reviews and oppositions and other challenges to the validity or enforceability of such Xencor General Patents. Xencor shall keep Aimmune generally informed of the status of Xencor General Patents upon Aimmune's request reasonable request from time-to-time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 9.3.2 removes the requirement for Aimmune to obtain Xencor's prior written approval for certain actions related to Xencor Product Specific Patents, Aimmune Patents, and Joint Collaboration Patents. This directly contradicts section 9.3.1, which grants Xencor 'sole right' over actions concerning Xencor General Patents. The removal of the approval requirement in 9.3.2 creates a conflict because it allows Aimmune to take actions regarding patents that may overlap with or impact Xencor's exclusive rights over Xencor General Patents as described in 9.3.1. This constitutes a strong contradiction.
